title: Rustic Crispy Venezuelan Dough Flatbread
description: A crispy and delicious Venezuelan flatbread made with a long-fermentation dough, perfect for snacking or as a side dish. This recipe features a blend of Mediterranean-friendly flavors and a crispy texture.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
In a separate bowl, proof the yeast by mixing it with the warm water and letting it sit for 5 minutes.
Add the yeast mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle and brush with olive oil.
Bake the flatbreads for 15-20 minutes or until crispy and golden brown.
Let the flatbreads cool for a few minutes before serving.
